cli

Class FlagRule

  • java.lang.Object
    • cli.FlagRule

  • public class FlagRule
extends java.lang.Object
    A stateful POJO (Plain Old Java Object) designed to represent a specific command-line flag definition and its subsequent parsed state.

    This class acts as a data container and state tracker. It maintains the configuration (name, type, requirements) and captures the results of the lexical analysis, including associated values and handled status.

    Since:
    26 February 2026
    Version:
    1.0
    Author:
    Trevor Maggs

      Constructors 
      Constructor Description
      FlagRule(java.lang.String name, FlagType type)
      Constructs a new flag rule based on POSIX or GNU conventions.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      void addValue(java.lang.String value)
      Associates a non-null value with this command flag.
      boolean expectsArgument()
      Returns whether this flag requires or permits at least one argument.
      boolean expectsSeparator()
      Checks if this flag is bound by the value separator (=) rule, according to the defined constraints.
      java.util.List<java.lang.String> getAllValues()
      Retrieves all values currently associated with this flag rule.
      java.lang.String getFlagName()
      Retrieves the name of this flag.
      FlagType getFlagType()
      Retrieves the type of this flag.
      int getSize()
      Returns the number of values stored within this flag.
      java.lang.String getValue(int index)
      Returns a value from the specified position in the list.
      boolean hasSeparator()
      Queries whether a value separator (=) was detected for this flag.
      boolean hasValueAssigned()
      Checks if this flag has any values assigned to it.
      boolean isFlagHandled()
      Indicates whether this flag has been marked as handled during the current parsing phase.
      boolean isLongFlag()
      Returns true if the flag is a long flag type, for example: --verbose.
      boolean isRequired()
      Indicates whether this flag is mandatory for the current parsing operation, according to the defined constraints.
      boolean isShortFlag()
      Returns true if the flag is a short flag type, for example: -v.
      void setFlagHandled()
      Sets a status indicating that this flag has been handled, including processing any associated arguments or values.
      void setSeparator(boolean found)
      Records the presence of a value separator (=) detected during the lexical parsing of this specific flag instance.
      java.lang.String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this flag rule, detailing its name, type, current parsing status, and any associated values.

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, wait, wait, wait

    • Field Detail

      • flagType

        private final FlagType flagType

      • longFlag

        private final boolean longFlag

      • flagName

      • values

      • handled

        private boolean handled

      • separator

        private boolean separator

    • Constructor Detail

      • FlagRule

        public FlagRule(java.lang.String name,
                FlagType type)
         throws java.text.ParseException
        Constructs a new flag rule based on POSIX or GNU conventions.
        Parameters:
        name - the flag name, including leading dashes. For example: "-v" or "--verbose"
        type - the policy for argument and separator handling
        Throws:
        java.text.ParseException - if the flag format is invalid or if a short flag exceeds one character

    • Method Detail

      • getFlagName

        public java.lang.String getFlagName()
        Retrieves the name of this flag.
        Returns:
        the flag name

      • getFlagType

        public FlagType getFlagType()
        Retrieves the type of this flag.
        Returns:
        the FlagType, which defines this flag

      • addValue

        public void addValue(java.lang.String value)
        Associates a non-null value with this command flag.
        Parameters:
        value - the string value parsed from the command line to be associated with this rule

      • hasValueAssigned

        public boolean hasValueAssigned()
        Checks if this flag has any values assigned to it.
        Returns:
        true if the flag has at least one assigned value, false otherwise

      • getSize

        public int getSize()
        Returns the number of values stored within this flag.
        Returns:
        the count of assigned values

      • isShortFlag

        public boolean isShortFlag()
        Returns true if the flag is a short flag type, for example: -v.
        Returns:
        true if it is a short flag

      • isLongFlag

        public boolean isLongFlag()
        Returns true if the flag is a long flag type, for example: --verbose.
        Returns:
        true if it is a long flag

      • expectsArgument

        public boolean expectsArgument()
        Returns whether this flag requires or permits at least one argument.
        Returns:
        true if an argument is expected or optional, false otherwise

      • setFlagHandled

        public void setFlagHandled()
        Sets a status indicating that this flag has been handled, including processing any associated arguments or values.

      • isFlagHandled

        public boolean isFlagHandled()
        Indicates whether this flag has been marked as handled during the current parsing phase.
        Returns:
        true if the processing of this flag is complete, otherwise false

      • isRequired

        public boolean isRequired()
        Indicates whether this flag is mandatory for the current parsing operation, according to the defined constraints.
        Returns:
        true if the flag is required, otherwise false

      • expectsSeparator

        public boolean expectsSeparator()
        Checks if this flag is bound by the value separator (=) rule, according to the defined constraints.
        Returns:
        true if the value separator is required or permitted, otherwise false

      • setSeparator

        public void setSeparator(boolean found)
        Records the presence of a value separator (=) detected during the lexical parsing of this specific flag instance.

        This state is used by the parser to validate syntax constraints (e.g., SEP_REQUIRED) on a per-occurrence basis.

        Parameters:
        found - true if a separator was explicitly present in the token, false otherwise

      • hasSeparator

        public boolean hasSeparator()
        Queries whether a value separator (=) was detected for this flag.
        Returns:
        true if a separator was present in the command line, otherwise false

      • getValue

        public java.lang.String getValue(int index)
        Returns a value from the specified position in the list.
        Parameters:
        index - the index of the value to retrieve
        Returns:
        the value at the specified position
        Throws:
        java.lang.IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the index is out of bounds

      • getAllValues

        public java.util.List<java.lang.String> getAllValues()
        Retrieves all values currently associated with this flag rule.
        Returns:
        an unmodifiable List containing all assigned string values, or an empty list if no values have been associated with this flag

      • toString

        public java.lang.String toString()
        Returns a string representation of this flag rule, detailing its name, type, current parsing status, and any associated values.
        Overrides:
        toString in class java.lang.Object
        Returns:
        a formatted string containing the flag's definition and parsed state
